verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I membrane protein explicitly localized to both plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae. Its TM domain confers its own membrane anchor; when present at the PM (as documented), it presents an extracellular face. However, its primary functional role is in ER–Golgi vesicular trafficking, making the PM a secondary cycling pool rather than stable residence. Dual-localization biology with documented PM presence qualifies for contextual accessibility.
